business.headline: OnEMI Tech IPO far from fully booked; brokerage flags key risksOnEMI Technology Solutions' IPO remained underbooked on the second day, with weak retail and NII demand. The muted grey market premium and brokerage warnings have kept the issue in a cautious zone.
NSE introduces electronic gold receipts to digitise gold tradingEach EGR is backed by real gold, ensuring that investors still own physical value, just without the need to store it themselves.

Govt shields fuel buyers, rules out relief for oil PSUs amid crude spike: ReportThe government has ruled out compensating state-run fuel retailers while keeping retail petrol, diesel and LPG prices steady. The move shifts part of the cost to bulk and industrial users as inflation concerns stay in focus.

FIFA World Cup 2026 faces broadcast uncertainty in India, ChinaIn India, a Reliance-Disney joint venture has offered $20 million for 2026 World Cup broadcast rights, a fraction of FIFA's ask, which was not acceptable to soccer's global governing body, two sources told Reuters on Monday.

Sensex, Nifty rise on election boost, gains capped by weak rupeeMarkets closed higher on Monday after early gains drew support from state election trends and earnings optimism. The rally lost steam later as a weak rupee, firmer crude and profit booking kept investors cautious.

Are rising fuel costs and global risks reshaping the aviation sector?With fuel costs climbing and geopolitical risks increasing, the aviation sector is feeling the strain, but is this a short-term phase or a lasting shift?

Your mobile number is key to your finances. Here's the upside and riskIn India, the mobile number is becoming the key identifier across banking, payments, loans and insurance. That convenience is widening access, but it is also sharpening concerns around fraud, privacy and control.

Explained: Why BHEL share price jumped 10% to hit upper circuit todayBHEL stock: BHEL shares hit the 10% upper circuit after the company reported a sharp March quarter earnings beat. The rally was driven by stronger profit, better margins, solid revenue growth and a dividend recommendation.
8th Pay Commission update: Where things stand after 6 monthsSix months after its formation, the 8th Pay Commission is moving steadily, but how far has it really progressed so far? Let's have a look.

Sensex, Nifty get thumbs up as BJP leads in Bengal, markets gainThe BSE Sensex was up 334.71 points, or 0.44%, at 77,248.21 as of 12:44 pm, while the Nifty 50 gained 103.90 points, or 0.43%, to trade at 24,101.45 as of 12:30 PM.

Are election results behind today's fall in gold prices?Gold prices slipped on Monday, and one big question is on everyone's mind, i.e., are election results the reason behind the dip, or is there more at play in the market?

Why India's smaller cities are the next premium real estate goldmineTier II and III cities are emerging as the next hub for premium real estate growth, driven by infrastructure spending and stronger connectivity. The shift points to a long-term move towards high-value housing beyond metros as incomes and aspirations rise.

How this woman built a Rs 1 crore net worth by 28 before her weddingJust days before stepping into a new phase of life, a 28-year-old woman hit a goal many spend decades chasing—a Rs 1 crore net worth. And she did it without complex strategies or expert help.

Sensex jumps 700 points: Why is stock market rising today?The BSE Sensex jumped 896.70 points, or 1.17%, to 77,810.20 as of 9:50 am, while the Nifty 50 rose 262.70 points, or 1.09%, to 24,260.25 as of 9:50 am.
Sensex, Nifty jump over easing crude oil prices; Maruti leads gainsThe S&P BSE Sensex was up 778.78 points to 77,692.28, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 223.80 points to 24,221.35 as of 9:35 am.

OnEMI Technology IPO Day 2: Check latest subscription, GMP, listing detailsThe public issue was subscribed 0.63 times by the end of Day 2. While retail participation remained modest at 0.17 times, demand from qualified institutional buyers (QIBs) stood strong at 1.51 times, indicating confidence from large investors. The non-institutional investor (NII) segment was subscribed 0.53 times.

Who is Rohit Jain, the new RBI deputy governor?The government has appointed Rohit Jain as an RBI deputy governor for a three-year term from May 2026. The move brings a longtime insider with banking supervision experience into a key role as portfolios are set to be reshuffled.

Thermos recalls 8.2 million containers after stoppers caused vision lossThermos has recalled 8.2 million food jars and bottles after stoppers unexpectedly ejected and injured users. The defect has been linked to pressure build-up, with 27 incidents reported and three cases of permanent vision loss.

Amid Hormuz closure, OPEC+ raises oil output quotas, no mention of UAE withdrawalSeven OPEC+ countries will raise oil output targets by 188,000 barrels per day in June, the third consecutive monthly increase, OPEC+ said in a statement after an online meeting. The increase is the same as that agreed for May minus the share of the United Arab Emirates, which left the group on May 1.
AI is helping you invest. But should you trust it?From picking stocks to timing trades, AI is becoming your new investment guide. The real question is, should you trust it with your money?

entertainment.headline: Jana Nayagan producer reacts to Vijay's election win; fans demand CM title card Jana Nayagan producer Venkat K Narayana congratulated Vijay after the latter's political party, Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam surged past 100 seats in Tamil Nadu. Narayana shared a statement on Vijay's performance in the election.

Prakash Raj on Vijay's election victory: Society is fan-dominated, not political Prakash Raj reacted to Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam leading the Tamil Nadu election results. While congratulating the winners, he raised concerns about fan-driven politics.

The Devil Wears Prada 3: Cast reveals two conditions for a possible third film Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway and Emily Blunt opened up on the possibility of a third The Devil Wears Prada film and also reflected on the core cast returning.

Vicky Kaushal in Aamir Khan's 3 Idiots sequel as fourth 'idiot'? What we know Vicky Kaushal is reportedly in talks to join Aamir Khan's 3 Idiots sequel as a fourth "idiot," adding a fresh twist to the beloved franchise. Read on to know more.

Chiranjeevi, Suriya congratulate Vijay as TVK nears victory in Tamil Nadu Actor-turned-politician Vijay received congratulatory messages from Chiranjeevi, Mahesh Babu, Suriya and others as TVK secured a majority in the Tamil Nadu assembly elections.

Hugh Jackman ready to take blame for divorce, seeks peace with ex-wife Deborra Actor Hugh Jackman is reportedly trying to make peace with ex-wife Deborra-Lee Furness by accepting blame for their marriage breakdown. His effort comes before his daughter Ava's 21st birthday, as the actor hopes the family can celebrate the occasion together.

Trisha Krishnan gets special birthday poster from Karuppu team ahead of release Makers of her upcoming film, Karuppu, unveiled a special birthday poster for actor Trisha Krishnan on May 4, offering a vibrant glimpse of her character, Preethi.
Deepika Padukone to miss Met Gala 2026 amid film shoots Deepika Padukone will not attend the Met Gala this year because of filming commitments. Met Gala to take place on May 5 (IST).

Cameron Diaz-Benji Madden welcome third child, and he has a unique name Actor Cameron Diaz and singer Benji Madden have welcomed their third child, a baby boy. The couple shared the news on social media.

Imran Khan on co-parenting: It's a privilege, not a duty Imran Khan said parenting is a privilege, not a duty, calling the time he spends with his child a source of joy. He also emphasised co-parenting and emotional security, stressing that children thrive when both parents share responsibility.

Absolute nonsense: Kiara Advani denies asking for cuts in Toxic intimate scenes Actor Kiara Advani has denied reports that she asked for intimate scenes with Yash in Toxic to be cut. Her response came as the film awaits a new release date after its postponement.
Spike Lee defends Michael timeline amid row over missing abuse claims Spike Lee defended the timeline of Michael Jackson's biopic, Michael against criticism over its omission of abuse allegations.

Vijay believed he'd be chief minister: Father reacts as TVK shakes Tamil Nadu Vijay's father SA Chandrasekhar said he was proud as TVK surged ahead in the Tamil Nadu election count. The early lead in the poll result triggered celebrations, tighter security and talk of a landmark political debut.

Did Kareena appear in Kaho Naa... Pyaar Hai? Old clip goes viral A resurfaced climax clip from Kaho Naa... Pyaar Hai has prompted viewers to claim Kareena Kapoor appears briefly in the final cut of the film. The moment has revived discussion around the film's continuity lapses and Kareena's exit after initially being part of the project.

No Bezos link: The Devil Wears Prada 2 writer clears air on billionaire subplot The Devil Wears Prada 2 writer Aline Brosh McKenna has dismissed claims that a subplot in the sequel is based on entrepreneur Jeff Bezos.

Is Kangana ready to marry Rahul Gandhi if he joins BJP? Read what she said According to a viral post, Kangana Ranaut said she'd marry Rahul Gandhi if he joined the BJP. Read to know what the actor said.

Are Janhvi and Shikhar getting married? Boney Kapoor sets the record straight Reports have surfaced that Janhvi Kapoor and Shikhar Pahariya are set to get married. The couple, who began dating early in life, reportedly broke up in 2016 before reconciling in 2022.

Nani, Mahesh Babu, celebs congratulate Vijay as TVK nears majority in debut poll Film personalities congratulated Vijay after early counting trends showed TVK leading in Tamil Nadu. The reactions gathered pace as his debut election performance pushed the party close to a majority.

Uttapam on MasterChef Australia: Contestant's tribute to home wins hearts Indian-origin contestant Kanika Gadyok served uttapam with chutneys and sambar on MasterChef Australia. Her quick batter innovation and tribute turned a simple dish into a standout moment.

general news.headline: BJP matches Congress's peak under Indira. Saffron surge from Kutch to KibithuWith the victory in West Bengal and Assam, the BJP-led NDA is governing 21 states and Union Territories. The spread matches its short-lived 2018 peak and equals the peak Congress reached under Indira Gandhi in the 1970s. The BJP now governs 72% of India's population.

3 Indians injured in Fujairah as Iran launches fresh missile, drone attack on UAEThe United Arab Emirates said Iran launched four cruise missiles during the latest attack. According to the defence ministry, three were intercepted by air defence systems, while the fourth fell into the sea.

BJP takes Bengal, TVK surge in Tamil Nadu, UDF back in Kerala, NDA sweep in AssamThe BJP will form its first government in West Bengal and has secured a third consecutive term in Assam. In Tamil Nadu, Vijay's TVK has marked a striking electoral debut, while in Kerala, the UDF has staged a comeback after a decade out of power.

12 years ago, Amit Shah made a promise to MamataThe BJP has comprehensively defeated the Trinamool Congress in West Bengal, ending its 15-year rule. The result caps a years-long grassroots push, and has revived a statement that BJP leader Amit Shah made in 2014, vowing to uproot Mamata Banerjee's party from Bengal.

The many choices before Vijay in Tamil Nadu. Who will he pick?Actor-turned-politician Vijay made a thunderous debut in Tamil Nadu, a state that has a long history of electing film stars as leaders. Vijay had promised change amidst the Dravidian duopoly. However, if the TVK fails to reach the halfway mark, what options does it have?

PM Modi's high-voltage Bengal push blends scale, symbolism, and strategyThe BJP has centred its West Bengal election campaign on Narendra Modi's wide-ranging outreach across the state. The effort has turned the contest into a larger battle over identity, welfare and competing political narratives.

BJP's big breakthrough in Kerala, wins 3 assembly seats in ThiruvananthapuramBJP scripted a three-seat breakthrough in Kerala but remained a distant third as the Congress-led UDF swept back to power with a landslide victory.

Vijay's driver's son Sabarinathan wins Virugambakkam for TVKR Sabarinathan, son of Vijay's longtime aide Rajendran, has won the Virugambakkam Assembly seat for TVK. The result turns a viral campaign moment into a marker of the party's accessibility, loyalty and grassroots rise.

Did Mamata lose Bengal election on August 9, 2024, itself?Women, the force behind Mamata Banerjee, seem to have deserted the Trinamool Congress for the BJP. The RG Kar rape and murder hit society's conscience and no amount of dole would repair the hurt. That's evident in the results of the West Bengal Assembly election on Monday.

BJP's Silent Machine: How groundwork powered Bengal surgeThe BJP's rise in West Bengal was driven by a calibrated ground operation spanning data, welfare and outreach. It showed how localised messaging and booth-level execution can reshape electoral outcomes.

Godrej Industries lays out 2031 growth ambitionsThe purpose statement, 'Crafting tomorrow since 1897', celebrates the Godrej Group's legacy while clearly positioning the new, refreshed Godrej Industries for the future.

Rain and hail on its way: May to grow more erratic, temperatures to dropRainfall sweeps across large parts of India, bringing partial relief from heatwave conditions as IMD warns of thunderstorms, hailstorms and gusty winds in multiple states.

Manifesting a mandate: Filmography of actor Vijay as the biography of CM VijayVijay's political entry feels organic, shaped over years through films that reflected social concerns, leadership, and reform, gradually transforming his screen persona into a credible real-life political identity.

After Bengal sweep, who will BJP pick as Chief Minister?After the BJP's decisive Bengal win, focus has shifted to who will step into Mamata Banerjee's shoes as chief minister. The choice will show whether the BJP prioritises Bengali identity, a woman leader or a seasoned organisational face.

Cooch Behar to Asansol, TMC workers chased and attacked; BJP urges restraintPost-poll violence was reported in several parts of West Bengal, with local Trinamool Congress leaders chased and attacked. This came as the BJP routed the Mamata Banerjee-led party in the Assembly election. BJP leader Arjun Singh urged party workers to maintain calm.

Live: BJP wins 200+ seats as Bengal and Bhabanipur slips through Mamata's fingersBengal Assembly Election Results 2026 Live Updates: The BJP, which has long set its sights on Bengal, appears to be on track for a breakthrough in what could mark a major political shift in the eastern state. The party has crossed the halfway mark of 147 seats and is now leading in over 170, well ahead of the Mamata Banerjee-led Trinamool Congress, which is around the 100 mark in early trends. Most importantly, the TMC's hold over the Presidency region appears to be slipping. The last time such a shift occurred under an incumbent government in Bengal, it marked the end of the Left's over three-decade rule. If these leads hold, it would signal a historic win for the BJP and a significant setback for the TMC, which has been in power in Bengal for the past 15 years.

PM's dhuti-panjabi attire for victory speech is a nod to BengalPrime Minister Narendra Modi made sure his attire spoke of the stunning victory the BJP pulled off in Bengal, a state they have wanted to capture for years. He sported traditional Bengali attire featuring a white panjabi (kurta) and dhuti (dhoti) at the BJP headquarters in Delhi during his address.

TVK giant-killer VS Babu brings down MK Stalin, ends DMK's Kolathur strongholdTVK candidate VS Babu defeated Chief Minister MK Stalin in Kolathur by 9,192 votes. The upset cracked a long-held DMK stronghold and sharpened focus on TVK's wider surge.

Lottery king Santiago Martin's family wins 2 seats in TN, 1 in PuducherryAadhav Arjuna, Leema Rose Martin, and Jose Charles Martin, the son-in-law, wife, and son of Santiago Martin, respectively, have won the election in Tamil Nadu and Puducherry.

Bengal today is free from fear, has seen poriborton, says PM ModiPM Modi, dressed in a traditional Bengali-style dhoti, called the BJP's 'double century' in Bengal historic while warning of the strictest action against infiltrators.

Chennai auto driver beats DMK and AIADMK heavyweights, wins Royapuram for TVKTVK's Vijay Dhamu, an auto driver, won Royapuram by defeating Subair Khan and D Jayakumar on Monday. The upset cracked a long-held North Chennai stronghold and underlined the party's striking debut in Tamil Nadu's 2026 Assembly election.

Congress-led UDF eyes best Kerala show ever as Left stares at irrelevanceThe Congress-led UDF is heading for its best performance since 2001, crossing 95 seats in early Kerala trends, with the LDF trailing toward a decisive defeat.

BJP in, Didi out. Saffron sweeps BengalThe BJP has wrested Bengal from Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress. The saffron side, which has longed for the eastern state for years, has pulled off a historic victory, marking the end of the TMC's 15-year reign.

Dump DMK? Congress rejects TVK tie-up talk after Vijay's father's outreachWith Congress leading in just a handful of seats in Tamil Nadu, an internal report to Rahul Gandhi and Mallikarjun Kharge flags support for TVK, even as Vijay's party continues to surge in Assembly trends.

sports.headline: Paisa vasool: How Rohit Sharma vowed fans with stunning comeback vs LSG IPL 2026, MI vs LSG: Harbhajan Singh said fans got full value for money as Rohit Sharma's 84 off 44 powered Mumbai Indians to a thrilling chase of 229 against Lucknow Super Giants at the Wankhede Stadium.

Helpless Rishabh Pant left praying for luck as LSG sink deeper after MI defeat IPL 2026, MI vs LSG: Rishabh Pant cut a helpless figure after the Lucknow Super Giants suffered their sixth straight defeat, going down to the Mumbai Indians by six wickets on Monday at the Wankhede Stadium.

Rohit Sharma roars back to keep Mumbai Indians alive in playoffs race IPL 2026, MI vs LSG: Rohit Sharma scored 84 off 44 balls as Suryakumar Yadav's Mumbai Indians beat Rishabh Pant's Lucknow Super Giants by six wickets on Monday, keeping their playoff hopes alive.

FIFA rejects Reliance-Disney $20M bid for 2026 World Cup India rights FIFA has rejected Reliance-Disney's $20 million bid for India's 2026 World Cup broadcast rights package. With time shrinking, millions of fans could miss the tournament unless broadcasters strike terms quickly.

MI's Raghu Sharma celebrates maiden IPL wicket with Jai Shri Ram note: Watch IPL 2026, MI vs LSG: Raghu Sharma marked his maiden IPL wicket with an emotional note after dismissing Akshat Raghuwanshi. The leg-spinner also impressed with figures of 4-0-36-1, underlining his steady rise from domestic grind to the Mumbai Indians setup.

IPL: Delhi prepare to unleash hell on CSK as Lungi Ngidi returns Lungi Ngidi is available for Delhi Capitals' home match against Chennai Super Kings after completing the mandatory break. His return could allow Delhi to field him with Mitchell Starc in a key contest between two sides level on points.

'BJP agent Messi': Fans have field-day after party sweeps Bengal elections The BJP swept the Bengal elections marking the end of the 15-year-rule of the TMC. Amidst the frenzy, the sports fans wondered, was this loss triggered by Lionel Messi's visit to Kolkata?

Nicholas Pooran shakes off KKR horror, decimates Mumbai Indians with 16-ball fifty IPL 2026, MI vs LSG: Nicholas Pooran silenced critics with a blistering 16-ball fifty, smashing 63 off 21 balls to power LSG after a poor run of form. He tore into MI's bowlers with fearless hitting before Corbin Bosch ended his stunning knock.

Who is Akshat Raghuwanshi? MP batter makes IPL debut against idol Rohit Sharma IPL 2026: Rishabh Pant confirmed Akshat Raghuwanshi's maiden IPL cap for Lucknow Super Giants against Mumbai Indians at Wankhede. The debut places a sought-after Madhya Pradesh batter on a bigger stage after strong domestic returns.

Vaishnavi Sharma stays grounded after T20 World Cup snub: Still a lot to learn Vaishnavi Sharma has taken her T20 World Cup snub in stride, saying she has plenty to learn and a long career ahead. The youngster remains focused on self-improvement and growth, drawing confidence from her domestic success and early international exposure.

Rohit Sharma is back: Mumbai crowd roars for legend after Suryakumar's message IPL 2026: The Wankhede crowd let out massive cheers as Rohit Sharma returned to the team's line-up on May 4. Stand-in captain Suryakumar Yadav announced major changes in MI's must-win game against Lucknow Super Giants.

Milan Rejoices as Inter secures 21st Scudetto with 2-0 win over ParmaInter beat Parma 2-0 to seal their 21st Scudetto and set off celebrations across Milan. The title completes a sharp recovery from last season's late collapse and keeps a domestic double in play.

Nandni Sharma trades lawyer, astronaut dreams for maiden India call-upNandni Sharma's childhood dreams of becoming a lawyer or astronaut gave way to cricket, a decision now paying off with a maiden India call-up after a standout season for Delhi Capitals in the Women's Premier League 2026.

Babar Azam's Zalmi try to recreate Rohit Sharma's celebration, fail hilariouslyPeshwar Zalmi beat Hyderabad Kingsmen to win the Pakistan Super League 2026. Zalmi captain Babar Azam tried to recreate Rohit Sharma's trophy celebration but ended up failing miserably.

Djokovic, Sinner, Sabalenka among stars disappointed over French Open prize moneyLeading players have challenged the French Open prize money offer before this month's tournament begins. The dispute centres on a falling revenue share and unresolved demands on representation, health support and pensions.

Will Rohit Sharma play MI vs LSG? Hitman's intense training session lifts hopesIPL 2026: Mumbai Indians are waiting on Rohit Sharma's fitness before facing Lucknow Super Giants at the Wankhede. His possible return comes as both sides fight to stay alive in the points table.

No MS Dhoni in CSK's next game after legend skips Delhi tripChennai Super Kings fans will have to wait longer for MS Dhoni in the Indian Premier League. The former CSK captain has not travelled with the team to Delhi to play the team's next game against Delhi Capitals.

RCB's Phil Salt flies back to England after injury: Will he return in IPL 2026?IPL 2026: Phil Salt has returned to England for scans on his finger injury after missing three matches for RCB. His absence has left Bengaluru reworking their top order at a crucial stage of IPL 2026.

Shedge stays grounded after Punjab Kings rescue act vs GTSuryansh Shedge struck 57 off 29 balls to lift Punjab Kings from 47 for 5 against Gujarat Titans. After the rescue act, he said his focus was on winning the trophy, not any race for places.

MI vs LSG preview: Mumbai, Lucknow fight for survival as elimination threatIPL 2026, MI vs LSG: Hardik Pandya's Mumbai Indians and Rishabh Pant's Lucknow Super Giants have their backs to the wall and will have their task cut out when they face each other on Monday at the Wankhede Stadium.

Shubman Gill continues social media banter, this time teases PBKS in Insta postIPL 2026: Shubman Gill followed Gujarat Titans' win over Punjab Kings with another teasing social media post. The post extended his pattern of post-match banter and drew fresh online reaction.

When Siraj's opening-over storm blew away PBKS top orderIPL 2026: Mohammed Siraj struck twice in the opening over to derail Punjab Kings against Gujarat Titans. The burst exposed how heavily Punjab's batting depends on a fast powerplay start.

Manchester United to hand Carrick permanent role after Champions League return?Manchester United beat Liverpool 3-2 at Old Trafford to secure Champions League qualification under caretaker boss Michael Carrick. The result has sharpened the debate over whether Carrick should get the job permanently.

World Relays: India end disappointing campaign with injury and DNFIndia's campaign at the World Athletics Relays ended on a disappointing note, marred by injuries, disqualifications and a string of failed baton exchanges. However, the mixed 4x100m relay team broke the National Record with a strong timing of 41.35 seconds.

KKR players smash cake on Andre Russell's face after win vs SRHIn perhaps the most hilarious match-winning celebration in the Indian Premier League this season, KKR's player turned coach Andre Russell had cake smashed all over his face after the team beat SunRisers Hyderabad. KKR beat 3rd placed SRH in Hyderabad on Sunday.

GT survive PBKS' last over scare, tame chase drags into tense Ahmedabad finishGT vs PBKS, IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans survived a tense final-over scare to beat Punjab Kings by four wickets in Ahmedabad, with Suryansh Shedge's rescue act and Punjab's late fight turning 163 into a gripping thriller.

Is this peak IPL? Mark Wood reveals bizarre story about team ownerEngland pacer Mark Wood narrated a bizarre story from his IPL stint, revealing how a franchise owner once offered to fly him in on a private plane to play a match in the tournament. Can you guess what IPL team and owner this could have been?

India will get the best out of Nandni Sharma: Jemimah Rodrigues backs young pacerJemimah Rodrigues backed young fast bowler Nandni Sharma to deliver for India in the Women's T20 World Cup later this year after she earned her maiden national call-up for the global event.

Manchester United vs Liverpool: Unconscious Alex Ferguson taken to hospitalFormer Manchester United manager Sir Alex Ferguson was taken to hospital after feeling unwell ahead of the team's Premier League clash against Liverpool on Sunday. Ferguson had fallen unconscious ahead of the game at Old Trafford.

Jannik Sinner boosts French Open hopes after historic Madrid Open title runMadrid Open: World No.1 Jannik Sinner clinched the title after defeating Alexander Zverev 6-1, 6-2 in the final in just 57 minutes. He will now aim to complete a Career Golden Masters when he competes at the Italian Open.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
